intro: PostHog and Mixpanel both show up under the product analytics category, but they solve slightly different jobs. PostHog uses usage-based pricing with free tier, while Mixpanel runs on pricing that starts at $20/mo (Growth, 10K MTUs) with free tier. Lock-in is low for PostHog and medium for Mixpanel. Transparency lands at 5/5 versus 4/5. PostHog fits teams working on want analytics + feature flags + session replay in one, while Mixpanel is a closer match when the job is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). Worth noting: PostHog is explicitly not for enterprise needing dedicated support; Mixpanel is explicitly not for simple projects. The honest trade-off: PostHog trades off on enterprise needing dedicated support — Amplitude is more enterprise; Mixpanel trades off on pre-product stage. On the plus side, PostHog highlights analytics + feature flags + session replay in one, while Mixpanel points to understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). PostHog's documentation also calls out open-source with self-hosted option. Mixpanel similarly notes data-driven product decisions.
quick_take: PostHog is for want analytics + feature flags +; Mixpanel is for understanding user behavior (funnels, retention); decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose PostHog if your project is want analytics + feature flags + session replay in one, usage-based billing at Usage-based, price decreases at scale matches your volume, you want to keep future migration cheap.
verdict_b: Choose Mixpanel if your project is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention), medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.

faq.a: PostHog uses usage-based pricing with free tier, and Mixpanel uses pricing that starts at $20/mo (Growth, 10K MTUs) with free tier. The pricing models are different, so a direct cheaper-than comparison depends on your volume and usage pattern.

faq.q: Can I migrate from PostHog to Mixpanel?
faq.a: Our data puts PostHog at low lock-in (open-source, self-hosted, raw data access via data warehouse), and Mixpanel at medium lock-in. Moving from PostHog to Mixpanel should be manageable, though you'll still need to replay integrations and re-test flows end-to-end.
Both score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. PostHog does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (5/5 vs 4/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is Mixpanel a good alternative to PostHog?
faq.a: Mixpanel is a reasonable alternative to PostHog when your workload leans more toward understanding user behavior (funnels, retention) than want analytics + feature flags + session replay in one. The pricing model shifts too — PostHog is usage-based pricing with free tier, Mixpanel is pricing that starts at $20/mo (Growth, 10K MTUs) with free tier — so expect the cost profile to change as well. One caveat: Mixpanel is explicitly not for simple projects, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
